speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Tullus Aufidius then had made new head?

text: Our swifter composition.

text: So then the Volsces stand but as at first,

text: Ready, when time shall prompt them, to make road

text: Upon ’s again.

text: That we shall hardly in our ages see

text: Their banners wave again.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Saw you Aufidius?

text: Against the Volsces, for they had so vilely

text: Yielded the town. He is retired to Antium.

text: Spoke he of me?

speaker: LARTIUS

text: To hopeless restitution, so he might

speaker: LARTIUS
text: At Antium.

text: I wish I had a cause to seek him there,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: To oppose his hatred fully. Welcome home.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Behold, these are the tribunes of the people,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: The tongues o’ th’ common mouth. I do despise them,

text: Speak briefly then,

speaker: SICINIUS
text: For we are peremptory to dispatch

speaker: SICINIUS
text: This viperous traitor. To eject him hence

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Were but one danger, and to keep him here

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Our certain death. Therefore it is decreed

speaker: SICINIUS
text: He dies tonight.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Now the good gods forbid

speaker: MENENIUS
text: That our renownèd Rome, whose gratitude

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Towards her deservèd children is enrolled

speaker: MENENIUS
text: In Jove’s own book, like an unnatural dam

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Should now eat up her own.

speaker: SICINIUS
text: He’s a disease that must be cut away.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: O, he’s a limb that has but a disease—

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Mortal to cut it off; to cure it easy.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: What has he done to Rome that’s worthy death?

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Killing our enemies, the blood he hath lost—

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Which I dare vouch is more than that he hath

speaker: MENENIUS
text: By many an ounce—he dropped it for his country;

speaker: MENENIUS
text: And what is left, to lose it by his country

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Were to us all that do ’t and suffer it

speaker: MENENIUS
text: A brand to th’ end o’ th’ world.

speaker: SICINIUS
text: This is clean cam.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Merely awry. When he did love his country,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: It honored him.

speaker: SICINIUS
text: The service of the foot,

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Being once gangrened, is not then respected

speaker: SICINIUS
text: For what before it was.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: We’ll hear no more.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Pursue him to his house, and pluck him thence,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Lest his infection, being of catching nature,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Spread further.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: One word more, one word!

speaker: MENENIUS
text: This tiger-footed rage, when it shall find

speaker: MENENIUS
text: The harm of unscanned swiftness, will too late

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Tie leaden pounds to ’s heels. Proceed by process,

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Lest parties—as he is beloved—break out

speaker: MENENIUS
text: And sack great Rome with Romans.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: If it were so—

speaker: SICINIUS
text: What do you talk?

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Have we not had a taste of his obedience?

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Our aediles smote! Ourselves resisted! Come.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Consider this: he has been bred i’ th’ wars

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Since he could draw a sword, and is ill schooled

speaker: MENENIUS
text: In bolted language; meal and bran together

speaker: MENENIUS
text: He throws without distinction. Give me leave,

speaker: MENENIUS
text: I’ll go to him and undertake to bring him

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Where he shall answer by a lawful form,

speaker: MENENIUS
text: In peace, to his utmost peril.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: Noble tribunes,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: It is the humane way: the other course

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: Will prove too bloody, and the end of it

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: Unknown to the beginning.

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Noble Menenius,

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Be you then as the people’s officer.—

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Masters, lay down your weapons.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Go not home.

speaker: SICINIUS
text:
Meet on the marketplace.
                 
                To
                 
                Menenius
                .
               We’ll attend you there,

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Where if you bring not Martius, we’ll proceed

speaker: SICINIUS
text: In our first way.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: I’ll bring him to you.

speaker: MENENIUS
text:
To
                 
                Senators
                .
                 
              Let me desire your company. He must come,

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Or what is worst will follow.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: Pray you, let’s to him.
